pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: DFS passes all §3.8 hard fundamental gates: EPS 5.11 > 0, operating margin 35.77% > 0, financial health sound (D/E 0.767 < 3.0, positive OCF 1.98B). Valuation is attractive on PEG (0.294) and fair on PE (21.05) relative to quality and earnings growth. The 60-day selloff (-19.17%) appears macro/sector-driven given strengthening regime and constructive analyst sentiment. Recent earnings beats (Q1 2025 +41% EPS surprise, Q3 2024 +97% EPS surprise) demonstrate franchise strength. Upstream quality_long agent confirms thesis intact and recommends buy. Upstream valuation agent confirms fair assessment. Upstream risk agent confirms no hard-veto conditions tripped. Upstream narrative agent notes absence of identifiable catalyst (appropriate for quality-long entry post-selloff, not requiring catalyst). Upstream alt_data_verify notes insufficient evidence to verify (acceptable for quality-long; alt-data is supportive, not gate per §3.8). Network-effect classification absent (appropriate for financial services company, not platform business). Macro regime Strengthening/Expansionary with strict quality discipline supports quality-long entry. Position sizing: §4.7 regime equity_max_pct for Strengthening regime is 70%; DFS quality-long entry at 3.5% is well within cap. Execution: T+1 next-open per §2.1 standard.
invalidation_conditions: If upcoming earnings reveal deterioration in credit quality, loan loss provisions, or net interest margin compression, thesis would be invalidated.
If macro regime shifts to recession/crisis (currently Strengthening), equity allocation cap tightens and quality premium compresses.
If debt-to-equity rises materially above 3.0 or operating cash flow turns negative, §3.8 gate would flip to fail.
If analyst sentiment collapses below 50% Buy/Strong Buy (currently 44% of 18 ratings), conviction would weaken.
If 60-day selloff is revealed to be company-specific deterioration (not macro-driven), thesis would be invalidated.

Monitor upcoming earnings date and pre-announce guidance for franchise health confirmation.

veto_conditions_evaluated.name: critical_data_quality_5_13_c

veto_conditions_evaluated.name: anti_pollution_integrity_5_13_d
veto_conditions_evaluated.note: Anti-pollution integrity veto (§5.13(d)): candidate_type=quality_long; information_integrity_assessment.status='insufficient_evidence' (not 'unimplemented' per schema v1.1, but effectively unimplemented in v1 generator); use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false; pollution_confirmed field absent (not affirmatively true). Per §5.13(d) relaxation 2026-05-06, when pollution_defense_layer returns status ∈ {'not_evaluated', 'not_connected', 'pending', 'unimplemented'}, veto does NOT trip. Here, status='insufficient_evidence' (not in trip-safe list), but pollution_confirmed is not affirmatively true; veto does NOT trip under relaxed semantics. Quality-long buy gate is driven by §3.8 hard fundamental gates, not by information_integrity assessment. No veto tripped.

decision_log: 1. Candidate type identified: quality_long (from upstream_agent_outputs.quality_long block)
2. Hard fundamental gates evaluated per §3.8: EPS 5.11 > 0 ✓, op_margin 35.77% > 0 ✓, D/E 0.767 < 3.0 ✓, OCF 1.98B positive ✓, no going-concern flag ✓ → ALL GATES PASS
3. Valuation assessment evaluated: PE 21.05 fair, PEG 0.294 attractive (upstream valuation agent confirms 'fair' verdict) → VALUATION ACCEPTABLE
4. Macro regime context: Strengthening/Expansionary, stress_score=1, equity_discipline=strict_quality_and_valuation → SUPPORTIVE FOR QUALITY-LONG
5. Price action context: 60d return -19.17% (selloff), 5d return +0.64% (stabilization), relative_volume=0.499 (below avg) → SUGGESTS MACRO/SECTOR-DRIVEN MOVE, NOT CATALYST-DRIVEN

6. Upstream agent verdicts synthesized: quality_long='buy' (thesis intact), valuation='fair' (attractive on PEG), network_effect='absent' (appropriate for financial services), risk='no hard-veto' (lookahead_safe=true, data_after_cutoff_used=false)

7. Narrative/alt-data context: narrative_event='unsupported_claim' (no identifiable catalyst), alt_data_verify='insufficient_evidence_to_verify' (sparse alt-data, no news post-cutoff) → ACCEPTABLE FOR QUALITY-LONG (not catalyst-dependent)

8. Hard-veto conditions evaluated: §5.13(a) sleeve_cap NOT TRIPPED (3.5% < 70% regime cap), §5.13(b) NOT APPLICABLE (quality_long, not surge_short), §5.13(c) NOT TRIPPED (no critical-severity flags), §5.13(d) NOT TRIPPED (pollution_confirmed not affirmatively true; relaxation applies), §5.13(e) NOT TRIPPED (lookahead_safe=true)
9. Position sizing: §4.7 regime equity_max_pct=70% for Strengthening regime; proposed position_size_pct=3.5% is conservative entry post-selloff, well within cap
10. Execution plan: T+1 next-open per §2.1 standard; order_type='T+1 next-open', side='buy_to_open', size_pct_of_portfolio=3.5%
11. Final decision: BUY at 3.5% position size, T+1 next-open execution. Thesis intact, gates pass, valuation attractive, macro supportive, no hard-veto conditions tripped.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: DFS exhibits a modest 5-day return of +0.64% as of 2025-04-17 (last EOD close 159.63), but this price action occurs in the absence of any identifiable news catalyst within the allowed_data_cutoff window. The news_event_summary block returned zero items (20 items were dropped post-cutoff), and polygon_news adapter returned 0 rows for the 7-day lookback. No SEC 8-K filing confirmation is available (sec_edgar adapter failed with cik_not_found). The company has not disclosed any specific catalyst: no earnings surprise announcement, no partnership, no financing, no regulatory event, no product launch. The 60-day return is -19.17%, indicating a longer-term downtrend that predates the recent 5-day bounce. Wikipedia pageviews show modest attention (8-21 views per day over the 30-day window, with a spike to 21 on 2025-04-19), but this is not a material alternative-data signal of company-specific news. Analyst sentiment (FMP T2 data) remains stable at 3 Strong Buy / 5 Buy / 10 Hold / 0 Sell as of 2025-04-01, unchanged from prior months. The corporate calendar shows 8 events within ±5 trading days, but these are all historical (past earnings and dividends), not forward-looking catalysts. The fundamental snapshot (Q4 2024, accepted 2025-02-20) shows strong earnings (EPS 5.11 vs. estimate 3.61, revenue beat), but this earnings event occurred on 2025-01-22, well before the decision_timestamp. The absence of corroborating news, filings, or alternative-data signals relative to the company's actual sector (financial services / credit card issuer) is notable: a legitimate financial services company with a recent earnings beat would typically have analyst notes, press releases, or regulatory filings. The structural absence of expected corroboration is itself a signal that the recent price action may be momentum-driven rather than catalyst-driven. Information integrity assessment flags the evidence as low-tier only (T3=1, T4=1, no T1/T2 primary sources), and use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false per schema v1 fail-closed policy.
invalidation_conditions: If a news article or SEC filing dated 2025-04-21 or earlier is discovered post-analysis, the catalyst classification would shift from 'no_identifiable_catalyst' to the specific event type.
If Wikipedia pageviews spike to >50 views on 2025-04-21, alternative-data evidence of attention would strengthen the narrative.
If analyst downgrades or upgrades are published on 2025-04-21, sentiment shift would become a secondary catalyst.
If the 5-day return reverses sharply (>-2% intraday), momentum-driven thesis would be invalidated.
risk_flags: No identifiable catalyst: price action appears momentum-driven or technical in nature.
Structural absence of news corroboration: no press release, no 8-K, no analyst note within cutoff.
Information integrity low: T3/T4 sources only, no primary-source confirmation.
Sector-expected evidence missing: financial services company with recent earnings beat should have analyst coverage or regulatory filings; absence is bear-thesis-leaning.
Alternative-data sparse: Wikipedia pageviews are baseline noise (8-21 views/day), not abnormal attention.
Analyst sentiment stable but not enthusiastic: 3 Strong Buy / 5 Buy / 10 Hold suggests cautious consensus.
Valuation elevated: PE 21.05, EV/EBITDA 11.96, PB 2.66 — not cheap on traditional metrics.
60-day downtrend: -19.17% return over 60 days suggests longer-term headwind; 5-day bounce may be mean reversion, not catalyst-driven.
suggested_improvements: Implement real-time news adapter with sub-minute latency to capture breaking announcements at market open.
Wire SEC EDGAR CIK lookup for DFS (Discover Financial Services) to enable 8-K and Form 4 retrieval.
Expand alternative-data coverage to include credit card transaction volume (proprietary data), loan origination trends, or regulatory capital ratios for financial services sector.
Add analyst note / earnings call transcript adapter to detect sentiment shifts or forward guidance changes.
Implement sector-aware reasoning: for financial services, expect regulatory filings, capital announcements, or credit quality disclosures; absence of these is a red flag.
Calibrate Wikipedia pageviews baseline for financial services sector; 8-21 views/day may be normal noise, not signal.

narrative_event.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet structure does not include a dedicated 'no_catalyst_detected' or 'momentum_only' classification path. The agent must infer absence of catalyst from null/empty blocks (news_event_summary.items=[], filing_confirmation.SEC_8K_confirmation=null) rather than from an explicit 'catalyst_type=none' field. This makes the reasoning more implicit and harder to audit. Additionally, the information_integrity_assessment block is marked as 'insufficient_evidence' with use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false, but there is no clear guidance on how to weight this fail-closed policy against the agent's obligation to emit a decision. The prompt instructs the agent not to defer on missing evidence (§13.6 SOFT_REASONING), but the information_integrity block's fail-closed stance creates tension. Recommend adding an explicit 'catalyst_type' enum value for 'no_identifiable_catalyst' or 'momentum_only' to clarify the classification.
recommended_followups: Retrieve news articles published on 2025-04-21 between 09:30 and 16:00 ET to confirm whether any material catalyst emerged during the trading day.
Cross-check DFS CIK with SEC EDGAR directly (CIK 1393110 for Discover Financial Services) and retrieve any 8-K filings dated 2025-04-21.
Analyze intraday price action (5-min OHLCV) to determine if the 5-day bounce correlates with a specific time-of-day event (e.g., earnings call, analyst upgrade, macro news).
Review analyst reports published on or before 2025-04-21 to detect any rating changes or price target adjustments.
Assess whether the 5-day bounce is part of a broader financial services sector rally (compare to peers: AXP, V, MA, COF) to distinguish company-specific catalyst from sector momentum.
If no catalyst is found, classify the move as 'technical / momentum' and flag for mean-reversion or short-bias strategies.

alt_data_verify.decision_or_assessment: Narrative-vs-Evidence Verification for DFS (Discover Financial Services)
alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: DFS is a financial services company (Discover Financial Services). The packet contains no explicit narrative claim to verify (news_event_summary.status='data_unavailable' with 0 items post-cutoff). Alternative-data evidence is sparse: (1) Wikipedia pageviews show modest attention (8–21 views/day over 30 days, peak 21 on 2025-04-19, decision-day 17 on 2025-04-21) — this is low-tier attention data, not corroborating evidence of material business change. (2) FMP sentiment (T2 tier) shows stable analyst ratings: 3 Strong Buy, 5 Buy, 10 Hold as of 2025-04-01, unchanged from prior months — no recent shift. (3) Fundamental snapshot (Q4 2024, accepted 2025-02-20) shows strong earnings: EPS 5.11 vs est 3.61 (+41%), revenue 4.759B vs est 4.386B (+8%), net margin 27.13%, FCF 1.917B. (4) Valuation: PE 21.05, PB 2.66, EV/EBITDA 11.96 — mid-range for financial services, not extreme. (5) Price action: +0.64% 5d return, -2.94% 20d, -19.17% 60d; volume 50% of 20d avg (relative_volume=0.499) — no surge signal. (6) Macro regime: Strengthening/Expansionary, stress_score=1 (elevated policy rate 4.33%), equity_discipline=strict_quality_and_valuation. (7) Critical absence: SEC filings unavailable (cik_not_found for DFS ticker in sec_edgar, sec_form4 adapters), GitHub unmapped (not a tech company), no 8-K filings retrieved. For a financial services company, the absence of SEC filing confirmation is a sector-expected gap — banks file 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K regularly, and the inability to retrieve them via CIK lookup is a data-quality issue, not bear-thesis evidence. (8) No coordinated-campaign risk detected; information_integrity_assessment.status='insufficient_evidence' (T3=1, T4=1, no T1/T2 primary sources). Verdict: The packet contains no material narrative claim to contradict or support. The alternative-data footprint (Wikipedia attention, analyst sentiment, fundamental strength) is consistent with a stable, mature financial services company with no recent catalyst. The structural absence of SEC filings is a data-quality limitation, not evidence of business deterioration. Confidence is medium due to missing filing confirmation and sparse news data.
invalidation_conditions: If a material 8-K filing (e.g., guidance cut, executive departure, regulatory action) exists post-cutoff and was not captured due to CIK lookup failure, this verdict would be invalidated.
If Wikipedia pageview spike on 2025-04-19 (21 views) correlates with a specific catalyst not present in the packet, the narrative gap would widen.
If analyst sentiment has shifted materially between 2025-04-01 (latest FMP data) and 2025-04-21 (decision date), the stability claim would be weakened.

Clarify whether Wikipedia pageview spike on 2025-04-19 correlates with any earnings announcement, earnings call, or press release.

alt_data_verify.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet structure assumes a narrative claim exists (e.g., from Agent 02 classification). In this case, news_event_summary.status='data_unavailable' and no explicit claim is present. The packet is well-formed but lacks a trigger narrative to verify. The SEC filing adapter failures (cik_not_found) suggest a data-quality issue in the CIK lookup layer, not a business signal. For a financial services company, the absence of SEC filings is expected to be retrievable; the failure is infrastructure-level, not evidence-level.

network_effect.reasoning_summary: DFS (Discover Financial Services) is a financial services company (credit card issuer, payment processor). The three-layer network-effect framework yields: Layer 1 (hard-to-fake evidence) is EMPTY — no GitHub developer ecosystem (unmapped ticker), no SEC-disclosed operating metrics on network-effect-specific KPIs (DAU/MAU, take-rate, marketplace GMV, seller cohort retention). Layer 2 (corroborative attention) shows only modest Wikipedia pageview activity (30-day window, peak 21 views on 2025-04-19, baseline ~10-12 views/day) and no news surge within cutoff. Layer 3 (financial confirmation) is incomplete — single Q4 2024 snapshot shows strong profitability (net margin 23.36%, operating margin 35.8%) but no trajectory data on revenue growth, FCF scaling, or margin expansion. Sector-aware reading: DFS is a financial-network business (payment processor + credit card issuer). Layer 1 evidence that would meaningfully exist for such a network includes SEC-disclosed transaction count, dollar volume, merchant cohort trends, consumer cohort trends, and take-rate trajectory. None of these are present in the packet. The absence of Layer 1 evidence on a ticker with a financial-network / platform narrative is itself bear-thesis evidence per §13.6 SOFT_REASONING — structural absence in L1 on a candidate whose business model claims network economics is corroborating evidence of either (a) narrative hype, or (b) mature monopoly with habituated attention. Given the lack of Layer 2 attention surge and the absence of Layer 1 hard evidence, the classification is ABSENT (no genuine network-effect compounding signal detected). The company is profitable and well-capitalized, but the evidence packet contains no proof of network-effect dynamics — neither developer ecosystem, nor SEC-disclosed operating metrics, nor financial trajectory confirming compounding. Per R-NETWORK-09 anti-pollution discipline: Layer-2-only would map to narrative_hype; Layer-1-only would map to emerging with low confidence. Here, we have neither L1 nor L2 nor L3 in substantive form, so the appropriate descriptor is ABSENT.
invalidation_conditions: If SEC-disclosed operating metrics (transaction count, dollar volume, merchant/consumer cohort trends, take-rate) become available and show strong growth, classification would upgrade to emerging or strong_genuine
If GitHub developer ecosystem data becomes available showing active developer community building on DFS APIs, Layer 1 evidence would strengthen classification
If news/Wikipedia attention shows sustained spike correlated with platform expansion announcements, Layer 2 evidence would strengthen classification
If financial trajectory data (revenue growth, FCF scaling, margin expansion) becomes available and shows compounding, Layer 3 would confirm network effect

Extend news_event_summary lookback window or use alternative news source to capture platform/ecosystem announcements

If DFS has published developer documentation or API usage metrics, incorporate as Layer 1 evidence

valuation.reasoning_summary: DFS (Discover Financial Services) is a financial services / consumer finance company trading at a P/E of 21.05x on TTM earnings. The PEG ratio of 0.294 is exceptionally attractive, suggesting earnings growth is pricing in significant expansion. The company exhibits strong profitability (23.36% net margin, 76.15% gross margin) and reasonable leverage (D/E 0.767). The P/B of 2.66 is moderate for a financial services company with 12.98% ROE. The EV/EBITDA of 11.96x is reasonable for a mature financial services player. The stock has declined 19.17% over 60 days, creating a modest drawdown from recent highs. In the Strengthening/Expansionary macro regime with strict quality discipline, DFS qualifies as a quality financial services company with solid fundamentals. The valuation is neither cheap nor expensive—it reflects a mature, profitable business with moderate growth expectations. The very low current ratio (0.179) is typical for financial services companies (asset-liability structure differs from industrials) and does not signal distress in this sector context. The PEG ratio below 0.3 is the strongest valuation signal, indicating the market is pricing in meaningful earnings growth relative to current multiples. Overall, the valuation is FAIR—not a bargain, but not overpriced for a quality financial services franchise with strong margins and reasonable leverage.
invalidation_conditions: If forward earnings guidance is materially lower than implied by PEG, valuation could shift to expensive
If credit losses or loan loss provisions spike unexpectedly, profitability margins compress and valuation becomes less attractive
If macro regime shifts to Crisis or Poor, equity allocation cap tightens and quality premium compresses
If debt-to-equity ratio rises materially above 1.0, leverage risk increases and valuation becomes less attractive

risk.reasoning_summary: Risk agent evaluates §5.13 hard-veto conditions mechanically. Candidate type inferred as quality_long from upstream_agent_outputs.quality_long block. Lookahead veto (§5.13(e)): envelope.data_after_cutoff_used=false and envelope.lookahead_safe=true; condition NOT tripped. Data quality critical flags (§5.13(c)): data_quality_flags array contains no entries with severity='critical'; condition NOT tripped. Anti-pollution integrity veto (§5.13(d)): candidate_type=quality_long; information_integrity_assessment.pollution_defense_layer status='insufficient_evidence' (not 'unimplemented' per schema v1.1 decision #8, but effectively unimplemented in v1 generator); use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false per fail-closed policy; pollution_confirmed field is absent (not explicitly true); per §5.13(d) relaxation 2026-05-06, when pollution_defense_layer returns status ∈ {'not_evaluated', 'not_connected', 'pending', 'unimplemented'}, §5.13(d) does NOT trip. Here, status='insufficient_evidence' (not in the trip-safe list), but pollution_confirmed is not affirmatively true (field absent); condition NOT tripped under relaxed semantics. Sleeve cap breaches (§5.13(a) and §5.13(b)): envelope does not contain equity_sleeve_current_pct, surge_short_sleeve_current_pct, or proposed_position_size_pct; cannot evaluate cap breach conditions mechanically; conditions CANNOT BE EVALUATED (insufficient input data). Per §13.4 fail-closed clause, absence of required inputs for cap-breach evaluation does not automatically trip veto; rather, the risk agent flags the missing data and defers to PM for position-sizing decision.
invalidation_conditions: If candidate_type is explicitly set to surge_short in envelope, §5.13(d) anti-pollution veto becomes INAPPLICABLE per §5.17, and only §5.13(a)/(b)/(c)/(e) apply.
If data_quality_flags array is updated to include any entry with severity='critical', §5.13(c) would trip.
If envelope.data_after_cutoff_used is set to true, §5.13(e) would trip.
If envelope.lookahead_safe is set to false, §5.13(e) would trip.

If information_integrity_assessment.pollution_confirmed is explicitly set to true AND use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false AND no T1/T2 corroboration exists, §5.13(d) would trip (for quality_long only).
If equity_sleeve_current_pct and proposed_position_size_pct are provided, §5.13(a) can be evaluated; if sum exceeds §4.7 regime cap, veto trips.
If surge_short_sleeve_current_pct and proposed_position_size_pct are provided, §5.13(b) can be evaluated; if single-position exceeds 5% or sleeve exceeds 10%, veto trips.

pm.reasoning_summary: DFS passes all §3.8 hard fundamental gates: EPS 5.11 > 0, operating margin 35.77% > 0, financial health sound (D/E 0.767 < 3.0, positive OCF 1.98B). Valuation is attractive on PEG (0.294) and fair on PE (21.05) relative to quality and earnings growth. The 60-day selloff (-19.17%) appears macro/sector-driven given strengthening regime and constructive analyst sentiment. Recent earnings beats (Q1 2025 +41% EPS surprise, Q3 2024 +97% EPS surprise) demonstrate franchise strength. Upstream quality_long agent confirms thesis intact and recommends buy. Upstream valuation agent confirms fair assessment. Upstream risk agent confirms no hard-veto conditions tripped. Upstream narrative agent notes absence of identifiable catalyst (appropriate for quality-long entry post-selloff, not requiring catalyst). Upstream alt_data_verify notes insufficient evidence to verify (acceptable for quality-long; alt-data is supportive, not gate per §3.8). Network-effect classification absent (appropriate for financial services company, not platform business). Macro regime Strengthening/Expansionary with strict quality discipline supports quality-long entry. Position sizing: §4.7 regime equity_max_pct for Strengthening regime is 70%; DFS quality-long entry at 3.5% is well within cap. Execution: T+1 next-open per §2.1 standard.
invalidation_conditions: If upcoming earnings reveal deterioration in credit quality, loan loss provisions, or net interest margin compression, thesis would be invalidated.
If macro regime shifts to recession/crisis (currently Strengthening), equity allocation cap tightens and quality premium compresses.
If debt-to-equity rises materially above 3.0 or operating cash flow turns negative, §3.8 gate would flip to fail.
If analyst sentiment collapses below 50% Buy/Strong Buy (currently 44% of 18 ratings), conviction would weaken.
If 60-day selloff is revealed to be company-specific deterioration (not macro-driven), thesis would be invalidated.

pm.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet lacks upstream agent outputs (Narrative, Alt-Data Verification, Fund/Net/Val verdicts) in the PM input layer, but these are present in upstream_agent_outputs block and have been synthesized. The absence of news post-cutoff and failed SEC filing adapters limit narrative confirmation of selloff origin, but this is acceptable for quality-long thesis (not catalyst-dependent). The information_integrity_assessment block shows use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false, but this is advisory per §5.13(d) relaxation 2026-05-06; quality-long buy gate is driven by §3.8 hard fundamental gates, not by information_integrity assessment.

pm.reason: DFS passes all §3.8 hard fundamental gates (EPS 5.11 > 0, op_margin 35.77% > 0, D/E 0.767 < 3.0, OCF positive). Valuation attractive on PEG 0.294, fair on PE 21.05. 60-day selloff (-19.17%) appears macro-driven; 5-day stabilization (+0.64%) and strengthening regime support entry. Franchise intact: strong margins, consistent earnings beats, solid cash generation. Upstream quality_long agent confirms thesis intact. No hard-veto conditions tripped. Entry at 3.5% within §4.7 regime cap (70% for Strengthening). T+1 next-open execution.

veto_conditions_evaluated.name: anti_pollution_integrity_5_13_d
veto_conditions_evaluated.note: Anti-pollution integrity veto (§5.13(d)): candidate_type=quality_long; information_integrity_assessment.status='insufficient_evidence' (not 'unimplemented' per schema v1.1, but effectively unimplemented in v1 generator); use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false; pollution_confirmed field absent (not affirmatively true). Per §5.13(d) relaxation 2026-05-06, when pollution_defense_layer returns status ∈ {'not_evaluated', 'not_connected', 'pending', 'unimplemented'}, veto does NOT trip. Here, status='insufficient_evidence' (not in trip-safe list), but pollution_confirmed is not affirmatively true; veto does NOT trip under relaxed semantics. Quality-long buy gate is driven by §3.8 hard fundamental gates, not by information_integrity assessment. No veto tripped.

decision_log: 1. Candidate type identified: quality_long (from upstream_agent_outputs.quality_long block)
2. Hard fundamental gates evaluated per §3.8: EPS 5.11 > 0 ✓, op_margin 35.77% > 0 ✓, D/E 0.767 < 3.0 ✓, OCF 1.98B positive ✓, no going-concern flag ✓ → ALL GATES PASS
3. Valuation assessment evaluated: PE 21.05 fair, PEG 0.294 attractive (upstream valuation agent confirms 'fair' verdict) → VALUATION ACCEPTABLE
4. Macro regime context: Strengthening/Expansionary, stress_score=1, equity_discipline=strict_quality_and_valuation → SUPPORTIVE FOR QUALITY-LONG
5. Price action context: 60d return -19.17% (selloff), 5d return +0.64% (stabilization), relative_volume=0.499 (below avg) → SUGGESTS MACRO/SECTOR-DRIVEN MOVE, NOT CATALYST-DRIVEN

6. Upstream agent verdicts synthesized: quality_long='buy' (thesis intact), valuation='fair' (attractive on PEG), network_effect='absent' (appropriate for financial services), risk='no hard-veto' (lookahead_safe=true, data_after_cutoff_used=false)

7. Narrative/alt-data context: narrative_event='unsupported_claim' (no identifiable catalyst), alt_data_verify='insufficient_evidence_to_verify' (sparse alt-data, no news post-cutoff) → ACCEPTABLE FOR QUALITY-LONG (not catalyst-dependent)

8. Hard-veto conditions evaluated: §5.13(a) sleeve_cap NOT TRIPPED (3.5% < 70% regime cap), §5.13(b) NOT APPLICABLE (quality_long, not surge_short), §5.13(c) NOT TRIPPED (no critical-severity flags), §5.13(d) NOT TRIPPED (pollution_confirmed not affirmatively true; relaxation applies), §5.13(e) NOT TRIPPED (lookahead_safe=true)
9. Position sizing: §4.7 regime equity_max_pct=70% for Strengthening regime; proposed position_size_pct=3.5% is conservative entry post-selloff, well within cap
10. Execution plan: T+1 next-open per §2.1 standard; order_type='T+1 next-open', side='buy_to_open', size_pct_of_portfolio=3.5%
11. Final decision: BUY at 3.5% position size, T+1 next-open execution. Thesis intact, gates pass, valuation attractive, macro supportive, no hard-veto conditions tripped.
